pages.title: About Cat - Cat Smith - Cat Smith
pages.text_excerpt: About Cat - Cat Smith - Cat Smith Skip to main content Cat Smith Working for Lancaster and Wyre Join Get Involved Donate About Data Protection Policy and Privacy Notice Expenses Contact Cat Contact Cat Register To Vote Local Team In Parliament Speeches and Questions Visit Parliament School Visits Local Priorities Proud and Accessible Communities Healthcare for Everyone Supporting Our Local and Rural Businesses Tackling the Climate and Ecological Emergency Get Support More Menu About Cat Cat was born in Barrow-in-Furness where she attended state schools. She moved to Lancaster as a teenager and graduated from Lancaster University in 2006. She is proud to call the city her home. Before being elected as an MP she worked for a national social work organisation and was a trustee for a local charity supporting victims of domestic violence. She was first elected as the Member of Parliament for Lancaster and Fleetwood in May 2015, winning the seat from the Conservative Party. Cat was re-elected in the Lancaster and Fleetwood constituency in 2017 and 2019 and after boundary changes in 2024 was re-elected to the constituency she currently represents of Lancaster and Wyre. In Westminster, she held various front bench roles between 2015 and 2021 including Shadow Secretary for Young People and Democracy, Shadow Deputy Commons Leader, Shadow Transport Minister and Shadow Women and Equalities Minister. She resigned from the Labour front bench during a reshuffle in 2021 to spend more time with her young family and in the constituency. When her son was in school, she went back to holding roles in Westminster, chairing the Petitions Committee from October 2023 until the last general election, and since 2024 she has Chaired the Procedure Committee in the House of Commons. As a girl she grew up attending Methodist youth groups and as such has a faith-driven quest for social justice, climate change action and fairness which is reflected in the issues she speaks out on. Cat speaks regularly on climate change issues; she fought against fracking at Preston New Road and the development of the huge gas storage facility at Preesall. Her commitment to youth engagement, democracy, political literacy, and animal welfare issues is often reflected in her campaigns in Lancaster and Wyre. Cat holds advice surgeries by appointment, and over the recess periods uses her time locally to host “Chats with Cat” which are informal drop-ins for constituents to meet Cat near where you live. To book an appointment please call 01524 566551. pages.title: Contact Cat - Cat Smith - Cat Smith
pages.text_excerpt: Contact Cat - Cat Smith - Cat Smith Skip to main content Cat Smith Working for Lancaster and Wyre Join Get Involved Donate About Data Protection Policy and Privacy Notice Expenses Contact Cat Contact Cat Register To Vote Local Team In Parliament Speeches and Questions Visit Parliament School Visits Local Priorities Proud and Accessible Communities Healthcare for Everyone Supporting Our Local and Rural Businesses Tackling the Climate and Ecological Emergency Get Support More Menu Contact Cat MP surgery Supporting constituents to help resolve their issues is one of the most rewarding aspects of being an MP. Although sadly I don’t have any formal authority over separately elected local councils or Government departments (eg the DWP, Passport Office, DVLA, Home Office, HMRC, Child Maintenance Service etc), where I believe I can try to help, I can make enquiries to support people to get the answers they need. I receive hundreds of emails every day and I always prioritise those from constituents, so please ensure that you have included your full name and address, including post code, in your email. I will not guarantee a reply if you do not put your full postal address on your email as there is a strict parliamentary protocol that MPs can only take up casework on behalf of their own constituents. Including a contact telephone number, any deadlines or reference numbers can also help speed up the support I am able to offer you. My casework team is based in Lancaster and when I’m at Westminster they are trained in talking to you about your concerns. If you want to contact me: You can email me: cat.smith.mp@parliament.uk You can telephone my constituency office on 01524 566551 or my Westminster Office 0207 219 6001. You can write to my office at 47 Scotforth Road, Lancaster LA1 4SA Please note due to strict Parliamentary protocol I am only able to make enquiries on behalf of my own constituents. To find out if I am your MP please visit https://www.parliament.uk/mps-lords-and-offices/mps/ Cat Smith Contact details Lancaster Labour, Scotforth Road, Lancaster, UK View on Google Maps Email: cat.smith.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by James Groves on behalf of Cat Smith and Lancaster and Wyre Labour Party; all at 47 Scotforth Road, Lancaster, LA1 4SA ©2026 Copyright Cat Smith. pages.text_excerpt: Local Priorities - Cat Smith - Cat Smith Skip to main content Cat Smith Working for Lancaster and Wyre Join Get Involved Donate About Data Protection Policy and Privacy Notice Expenses Contact Cat Contact Cat Register To Vote Local Team In Parliament Speeches and Questions Visit Parliament School Visits Local Priorities Proud and Accessible Communities Healthcare for Everyone Supporting Our Local and Rural Businesses Tackling the Climate and Ecological Emergency Get Support More Menu Local Priorities Proud and Accessible Communities See more Cat Attends Reproductive Justice Photo Exhibition! Communities Latest 01 May 2026 Right to Buy overhaul to safeguard social housing Communities Latest 28 April 2026 Cat Speaks At Quarry Appeal Climate Communities Latest 17 April 2026 Community groups and activities are the beating heart of our society, bringing the best of us together. But to create spaces fit for celebrating our communities, we need to take back control from London and have more local decision-making on public transport, housing, energy, skills, culture, and childcare. I’ve been tough on dodgy developers who try to build housing without any care for the impact on the local area, and challenged new developments in flood risk zones. I continue to challenge our train companies on the poor service they have provided, despite continually rising fares, and I have successfully lobbied our bus companies to restore the Sunday bus service to Knott End, increase services along St George’s Quay in Lancaster, and introduce fairer bus fares for Scorton and Forton. I’ve championed our local skate parks, youth services, green spaces and campaigned to save community assets like pharmacies, whilst challenging local government when their services are poor or work against residents, such as the poor state of weeding or City Council parking charge increases. Healthcare For Everyone See more Cycle training and walk to school programmes among £108 million active travel funding boost Health Latest 30 March 2026 Support for thousands with musculoskeletal conditions as government tackles inactivity Health Latest 28 March 2026 MHRA action boosts drive to phase out animal testing Health Latest 24 March 2026 From GPs, hospitals, dentists, and pharmacies to campaigns saving breast cancer screening services and local leisure centre facilities, I’m proud to fight to keep local healthcare and community health services open and available for everyone. I have continually campaigned for investment in our crumbling hospitals and A&E services. When a proposal was put on the table to merge the Royal Lancaster Infirmary with the Royal Preston Hospital to create a “superhospital” off the M6 near Preston, I successfully fought hard to keep hospital services in Lancaster. I was delighted that this resulted in the “superhospital” plan being ruled out, and a new state-of-the-art hospital in Lancaster being proposed instead. I am now campaigning for the government to give us the funds for this that were promised. Supporting Our Local and Rural Businesses See more Victims of workplace abuse will no longer be silenced Business Communities Latest 15 April 2026 Vodafone Mast in Lancaster to go Permanently Offline Business Communities Latest 02 April 2026 £1 billion to cut costs for businesses, drive growth and clean up UK roads Business Climate Latest 25 March 2026 Through their local and sustainable produce and services, I want to boost our small businesses champions and make sure their aspiration is rewarded. It takes guts and a huge leap of faith to go into business, and so I’m committed to giving them the best chance to thrive through cuts to business rates, making sure invoices get paid on time, and pushing to make Britain the high-growth, start-up hub of the world. From soaring energy bills, to changing retail habits, I am keenly aware of the need to support our high streets. I have supported hundreds of businesses with individual issues and I will keep advocating

pages.title: Cat's Record and Local Action for Residents - Cat Smith - Cat Smith
pages.h1: Cat’s Record and Local Action for Residents
pages.text_excerpt: Cat's Record and Local Action for Residents - Cat Smith - Cat Smith Skip to main content Cat Smith Working for Lancaster and Wyre Join Get Involved Donate About Data Protection Policy and Privacy Notice Expenses Contact Cat Contact Cat Register To Vote Local Team In Parliament Speeches and Questions Visit Parliament School Visits Local Priorities Proud and Accessible Communities Healthcare for Everyone Supporting Our Local and Rural Businesses Tackling the Climate and Ecological Emergency Get Support More Menu 23rd May, 2024 Cat’s Record and Local Action for Residents Serving as your Member of Parliament is a privilege. As we move into the General Election and I stand in the new Lancaster and Wyre area, I’m reminded of some of the local achievements we’ve made over the past few years: 🌍 Secured a fracking-free Lancashire 🏥 Campaigned for a new local hospital 👩‍⚕️ Saved local cancer services 🚌 Extended rural bus routes 🚂 Won funding to restore axed rail line 🏊‍♂️ Reopened leisure facilities 💧 Held United Utilities to account on sewage 🏃‍♀️ Raised money for food banks and hospices 🚓 Enhanced local area police patrols 🎫 Protected rail ticket offices from closure 🛍 Championed local small businesses 🏛 Preserved listed buildings and heritage 💰 Fought for environment disaster funds 📶 Fixed major mobile connectivity issues 💸 Won compensation for affected customers 🚮 Held park clean-up and community litter picks 👩‍⚖️ Expanded local court capacity 👵 Improved Pension Credit take-up 🚗 Stopped driving test centre closure 🩺 Changed hospital policies to free up GPs 🌊 Successfully lobbied for flood defence funding 📮 Challenged Royal Mail cuts & replaced boxes ❌️ Stood against quarry and landfill plans 🥳 Celebrated many community groups & events 🛣 Had major roads resurfaced & potholes fixed 🚧 Improved road safety and speed enforcement 🏡 Called for park home/alternative energy help 👷 Took dodgy developers to task 🏘 Objected to housing without public services 💐 Recognised local community champions 🙋‍♀️ Hosted Chats with Cat drop-in surgeries 👧 Spoke in schools on democracy & their futures 🚪 Enjoyed countless conversations on doorsteps 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Supported residents and local businesses This list is not mine, it bears the weight and power of thousands of your voices – and it’s an honour to represent them in Parliament. It’s a reminder of how lucky I am to have this opportunity. Thank you to all of you, especially those who will no longer be in the new constituency, such as the residents of Fleetwood and the Lower Lune Valley. As well as getting started on fixing our broken economy and public services, I hope to champion your priorities and aspirations for the next five years as your MP for Lancaster and Wyre. We live in a beautiful area. The hustle-and-bustle of an historic cultural city brimming with small independent businesses and a thriving higher education sector at the forefront of climate and health innovation, alongside the close-knit rural village life with renewable energy and sustainable agricultural vitality that keeps Britain powered, fed and connected – and all this on the doorstep of the cracking views across national landscapes like Morecambe Bay, the Forest of Bowland and the Lake District. It’s a vision of what Britain can aspire to be when we come together and harness our potential. So let’s roll up our sleeves and get the positive change our area deserves, and #VoteLabour! 🌹❤️🌹❤️🌹❤️🌹❤️🌹❤️🌹❤️🌹❤️🌹❤️🌹 ❤️🌹❤️🌹❤️🌹❤️🌹❤️🌹❤️🌹❤️🌹❤️🌹❤️ Cat Smith Contact details Lancaster Labour, Scotforth Road, Lancaster, UK View on Google Maps Email: cat.smith.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by James Groves on behalf of Cat Smith and Lancaster and Wyre Labour Party; all at 47 Scotforth Road, Lancaster, LA1 4SA ©2026 Copyright Cat Smith.
